articles.title: Four in five landlords unprepared for Renters’ Right Act
articles.summary: Four in five landlords (82%) are concerned about the impact of the Renters’ Rights Act, with one in two (44%) very concerned. Meanwhile, three-quarters (75%) of landlords say regulation has made them less likely to grow their portfolios, and half (49%) are planning to sell or reduce their stake within two months.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Landlord Alarm**: 82% of landlords express concerns about the Renters' Rights Act, with 44% significantly worried about its implications.
- **Portfolio Shrinkage**: 75% of landlords are discouraged from expanding their portfolios, and nearly half (49%) plan to sell or downsize within the next two months.

articles.title: Energy efficiency and EPC ratings become higher focus for tenants
articles.summary: Landlords must consider how to improve EPC ratings to attract tenants, even ahead of the new requirement to upgrade rental properties to a C rating by 2030. It comes as energy efficiency of properties has gained even greater focus as tenants look to save money after the energy price hikes caused by the Iran War.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords need to enhance EPC ratings to attract tenants in light of upcoming regulations mandating a minimum C rating by 2030.  
- Rising energy costs, exacerbated by the Iran War, have heightened tenant demand for energy-efficient properties to reduce expenses.

articles.title: Planning reform fails to inspire confidence – Landbay
articles.summary: Fewer than a quarter of the UK’s landlords feel planning reform will positively impact their business.
articles.ai_summary:
- Less than 25% of UK landlords believe that recent planning reforms will benefit their operations.
- Landbay highlights a significant lack of confidence in the proposed changes among property owners.

articles.title: Landlords warned a hidden leak could cost thousands
articles.summary: With more than half (58%) of hidden leaks originating outside a property, landlords must remember to focus on the maintenance of pipework outside as well as inside the property, leak detection specialist Aspect has warned.
articles.ai_summary:
- Over 58% of hidden leaks start outside properties, highlighting the need for landlords to prioritize external pipework maintenance.
- Leak detection experts warn that neglecting outside maintenance could lead to costly repairs for landlords.

articles.title: As RRA deadline looms, 5 top tips for landlords
articles.summary: From 1 May 2026, the Renters’ Rights Act will implement some of the biggest changes to the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales. The Act will aim to balance landlord and tenant rights and will impose new requirements and regulatory oversight on landlords.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Major Shift Coming:** The Renters' Rights Act, effective May 1, 2026, will transform the Private Rented Sector in England and Wales, enhancing tenant protections while balancing landlord rights.  
- **Prepare for Compliance:** As the deadline approaches, landlords must navigate new requirements and regulatory standards to ensure they meet the upcoming changes.

articles.title: 84% of landlords not ready for RRA – survey
articles.summary: More than eight in ten (84%) of landlords are unprepared for the Renters’ Rights Act go-live on May 1, with 58% at high risk of non-compliance according to new figures.
articles.ai_summary:
- 84% of landlords are unprepared for the upcoming Renters’ Rights Act effective May 1, raising concerns about compliance.  
- 58% of landlords face a high risk of non-compliance, highlighting significant gaps in readiness for the new regulations.

articles.title: Expansion no longer default response to rising demand as rental growth slows
articles.summary: Landlords are restructuring portfolios and financing in response to changing rental demand, rather than focusing on expansion, with the ONS announcement of a slowing in rental growth showing the need to ensure portfolios are right.
articles.ai_summary:
- Landlords are shifting their strategies towards restructuring portfolios and financing instead of defaulting to expansion due to changing rental demand.  
- The ONS highlights slowing rental growth, prompting landlords to reassess and optimize their property holdings.

articles.title: Landlords blamed for pushing first-time and local buyers out
articles.summary: Landlords and investors have been blamed for pushing first-time buyers and local buyers out of the market, according to a new survey.
articles.ai_summary:
- A recent survey highlights that landlords and investors are increasingly sidelining first-time and local buyers in the UK property market.
- Rising competition from these groups is contributing to affordability challenges for new homebuyers.

articles.title: Next government must stem landlord flow
articles.summary: Scotland's landlords have called for the next Scottish Government to formally recognise the private rented sector's (PRS) importance with a dedicated strategy.
articles.ai_summary:
- Scottish landlords urge the next government to acknowledge the critical role of the private rented sector (PRS) with a specific strategic plan.  
- The appeal highlights concerns over the increasing exit of landlords from the market, emphasizing the need for supportive policies.

articles.title: Landlords beware of registration requirement for tax advisor
articles.summary: Conveyancing solicitors and estate agency payroll teams are among those who could fall foul of new mandatory HMRC tax advisor registration requirements from May, facing penalties of up to £10,000.
articles.ai_summary:
- **New HMRC Regulations**: Starting May, landlords and related professionals (like solicitors and payroll teams) must register as tax advisors or face penalties up to £10,000.
- **Warning to Landlords**: Non-compliance could lead to significant financial repercussions, urging landlords to ensure they understand and meet registration requirements.

articles.title: Licensing doesn’t lead to higher rents – council’s claim
articles.summary: The council is expecting landlords to pay £694 per property without recovering the cost
articles.ai_summary:
- A council asserts that property licensing does not result in increased rents for tenants.
- Landlords face a £694 fee per property, which the council does not plan to offset through rent.

articles.title: Labour makes it clear – no rent controls
articles.summary: The pledge was made by Matthew Pennycook
articles.ai_summary:
- Labour's Matthew Pennycook confirms the party will not implement rent controls, signaling a shift in housing policy.
- This decision aims to address housing market concerns while balancing the needs of landlords and renters.

articles.title: Authority wants to stop “HMO landlord free for all”
articles.summary: The council is also drawing up detailed HMO guidelines
articles.ai_summary:
- Council plans to implement stringent guidelines for Houses in Multiple Occupation (HMOs) to regulate the current unchecked landlord practices.  
- Authorities aim to curb the "HMO landlord free for all" to enhance tenant safety and housing standards.

articles.title: Tenants tutored on maximising Renters Rights Act powers
articles.summary: The week-long campaign begins on Monday
articles.ai_summary:
- A week-long campaign starts Monday, educating tenants on leveraging their rights under the Renters Rights Act.  
- The initiative aims to empower renters, helping them understand and maximize their legal protections.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act provoking spike in S21 evictions
articles.summary: This is exactly what Paul Shamplina predicted
articles.ai_summary:
- The Renters Rights Act is leading to a sharp increase in Section 21 evictions, confirming predictions made by expert Paul Shamplina.
- This surge highlights the ongoing tensions in the UK rental market as landlords respond to new regulations.

articles.title: Rents rising ahead of Renters Rights Act – new data
articles.summary: The data comes from the lettings agency Hamptons
articles.ai_summary:
- Rent prices are increasing as landlords prepare for the upcoming Renters Rights Act, according to recent data from Hamptons.
- The rising rents highlight the growing pressure on tenants amidst changing housing regulations in the UK.

articles.title: Renters Rights Act will kill off the hobbyist landlord – opinion
articles.summary: We're just 10 days away from the Renters Rights Act becoming law
articles.ai_summary:
- The impending Renters Rights Act, set to be enacted in just 10 days, is poised to adversely impact amateur landlords.  
- Industry experts warn that the legislation may lead to a significant reduction in the number of hobbyist landlords in the UK property market.

articles.title: Gen Z entrepreneurs turn to property investment
articles.summary: Despite the red tape and extra tax poured on property investors in recent years.
articles.ai_summary:
- Gen Z entrepreneurs are increasingly diving into property investment, navigating challenges like red tape and heightened taxes.  
- This trend showcases a shift in investment strategies among younger generations, signaling their resilience in adapting to a tough market.

articles.title: Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group
articles.summary:
Sellers forced to cut asking prices, endure failed sales or withdraw from the market, reveals House Buyer Bureau’s Chris Hodgkinson.
The post Tough market for sellers, claims house buying group appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- House Buyer Bureau reports that sellers are struggling in a challenging market, resulting in price cuts, failed sales, and withdrawals.
- Chris Hodgkinson highlights the increasing pressure on sellers to adjust expectations amid current market conditions.

articles.title: HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations
articles.summary:
The number of cases referred to the Valuation Office Agency (VOA) is on the rise, it has been revealed.
The post HMRC ‘ramps up’ scrutiny of property valuations appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- HMRC is intensifying its oversight of property valuations, leading to a surge in cases referred to the Valuation Office Agency (VOA).  
- Increased scrutiny may signal tighter regulations and potential implications for property market stakeholders.

articles.title: Sleeping tenant caught on camera in £135k property listing photos
articles.summary:
The tenant is seen asleep in bed in the property listing photographs and video for a terrace property for sale in Penrith, Cumbria.
The post Sleeping tenant caught on camera in £135k property listing photos appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- A property listing for a £135k terrace home in Penrith, Cumbria, featured an unexpected surprise: a sleeping tenant caught on camera in the bedroom photos and video.  
- The incident raises questions about privacy and professionalism in real estate marketing.

articles.title: Referral fees are a ‘hidden tax’ on homebuyers
articles.summary:
Referral fees have operated as an “unseen surcharge’ on homebuyers, according to the Conveyancing Task Force.
The post Referral fees are a ‘hidden tax’ on homebuyers appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Referral fees are deemed an "unseen surcharge" on homebuyers, significantly inflating costs, warns the Conveyancing Task Force.  
- The analysis highlights referral fees as a "hidden tax" that adds financial strain to those purchasing homes in the UK.

articles.title: Lomond expands operation north of the border
articles.summary:
David Alexander, of Lomond, says Clyde Property Group will run its portfolio in the West of Scotland.
The post Lomond expands operation north of the border appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Lomond is expanding its operations into Scotland, with Clyde Property Group managing its portfolio in the West.  
- This strategic move marks a significant growth step for Lomond in the UK property market.

articles.title: Foxtons’ sales revenue plummets 35%
articles.summary:
Geopolitical headwinds have meant that new buyer activity was lower than expected, says Foxtons’ CEO Guy Gittins.

articles.ai_summary:
- Foxtons experiences a sharp 35% decline in sales revenue, driven by unexpected geopolitical challenges.
- CEO Guy Gittins attributes the downturn to reduced buyer activity amidst ongoing market uncertainties.

articles.title: Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices
articles.summary:
Price rises have hit fuel and transport hardest but are expected to soon spread to the rest of the economy.
The post Inflation rises as Middle East conflict drives up prices appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- Rising inflation, fueled by Middle East conflict, is significantly impacting fuel and transport costs.  
- Experts warn that these price increases are likely to ripple through the broader UK economy imminently.

articles.title: Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market
articles.summary:
The reality is that many new homes being built will never reach the market, says Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, Chief Executive of Alto.
The post Nine out of ten homes don’t reach the open sales market appeared first on The Negotiator.
articles.ai_summary:
- **Market Access Barrier:** Approximately 90% of newly built homes are not sold on the open market, highlighting a significant gap in property accessibility.  
- **Expert Insight:** Riccardo Iannucci-Dawson, CEO of Alto, emphasizes the implications of this trend for aspiring homeowners and market dynamics.
